name: Mines Trail
name_original: Mina de la Providencia, Barranco de San Martín, Barranco Rojo – ruta circular
description: Explore the moderate 9.0-mile Mina de la Providencia, Barranco de San Martín, Barranco Rojo circular route, featuring caves and a gorge.
region_intro: Hiking around Santa Engracia Del Jubera offers diverse terrain within the foothills of the Camero Viejo Mountains in La Rioja. The region is characterized by impressive ravines, steep rock formations, and the deep valley carved by the Jubera River. Much of the area lies within the Biosphere Reserve of the Valleys of Leza, Jubera, Cidacos, and Alhama, providing a preserved natural environment with varied flora and fauna. This landscape includes mid-sized mountains and historical sites like ancient lead mines and the ruins of Jubera Castle. Best hiking trails around Santa Engracia Del Jubera The most popular hiking route is The Island in Soto de Cameros, Fountain of the 3 spouts, Vulture Viewpoint – circular route, a 5.3 miles (8.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 46 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a significant elevation gain of 1177 feet (359 meters). Another top favourite among local hikers is Mines Trail, a moderate 9.0 miles (14.5 km) path. This trail explores the historical lead mines of Jubera, offering insights into the region's past. Local hikers also love the Cascada El Gallizo y San Vicente de Robres — circular desde Robres del Castillo, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 20 minutes. Hiking around Santa Engracia Del Jubera is defined by deep river valleys, steep rock formations, and historical mining landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Santa Engracia Del Jubera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Santa Engracia Del Jubera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Santa Engracia Del Jubera?
A: There are over 50 hiking trails around Santa Engracia Del Jubera, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and interests. The komoot community has highly rated these routes, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Santa Engracia Del Jubera offers several easy hiking trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1327377230/murillo-de-rio-leza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Murillo de Río Leza&lt;/a&gt; route is an easy 5.2 km path with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a relaxed walk. The region has 19 easy routes in total, many of which are suitable for families.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: While most routes are moderate, the region does offer some more challenging options. The landscape is characterized by impressive ravines and steep rock formations, particularly in areas like the Barranco de San Martín, which can provide a more strenuous experience. There is at least one difficult route available for those seeking a greater challenge.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: Santa Engracia Del Jubera's wild and rural environment, combined with its extensive network of trails, generally makes it a dog-friendly area. However, it's always advisable to check specific trail regulations or local signage, especially within the Biosphere Reserve, and keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Santa Engracia Del Jubera are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989616966/the-island-in-soto-de-cameros-fountain-of-the-3-spouts-vulture-viewpoint-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Island in Soto de Cameros, Fountain of the 3 spouts, Vulture Viewpoint – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 8.6 km loop offering diverse scenery. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1207235272/cascada-el-gallizo-y-san-vicente-de-robres-circular-desde-robres-del-castillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada El Gallizo y San Vicente de Robres — circular desde Robres del Castillo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there trails that lead to waterfalls or other natural water features?
A: Yes, you can find trails that lead to beautiful natural water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1207235272/cascada-el-gallizo-y-san-vicente-de-robres-circular-desde-robres-del-castillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada El Gallizo y San Vicente de Robres — circular desde Robres del Castillo&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, will take you to the Cascada El Gallizo. The Jubera River itself carves a deep valley, providing picturesque scenery and opportunities to explore its course.

Q: Can I explore historical sites while hiking in the area?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. You can hike to the ruins of Jubera Castle, a 10th-century landmark perched above the Jubera River. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989465866/mines-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mines Trail&lt;/a&gt; explores the ancient lead mines of Jubera, offering a unique glimpse into the region's industrial past.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes and viewpoints can I expect to see?
A: Santa Engracia Del Jubera is known for its diverse and rugged natural landscape. You'll encounter impressive ravines, steep rock formations, and the deep valley carved by the Jubera River. Much of the area is within the Biosphere Reserve of the Valleys of Leza, Jubera, Cidacos, and Alhama, offering preserved natural environments. Look out for viewpoints like the Vulture Viewpoint mentioned in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989616966/the-island-in-soto-de-cameros-fountain-of-the-3-spouts-vulture-viewpoint-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Island in Soto de Cameros&lt;/a&gt; route, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3753514" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leza Canyon Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Santa Engracia Del Jubera?
A: The diverse terrain and protected natural environments make Santa Engracia Del Jubera suitable for hiking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Winter hiking can also be rewarding, especially for birdwatching, as the area is part of a Biosphere Reserve with abundant flora and fauna.

Q: Is parking available at trailheads, and how accessible is the area by public transport?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead vary, many routes in rural areas like Santa Engracia Del Jubera typically have designated or informal parking spots near their starting points. Accessibility by public transport can be limited in this rugged region, so planning your journey in advance and considering car travel for reaching trailheads is often recommended.

Q: What other attractions can I visit near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the trails, the region offers several points of interest. You can explore historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2706737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Clavijo Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6164621" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguas Mansas Castle&lt;/a&gt;. For natural wonders, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1255782" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pladortes Ichnite Site&lt;/a&gt;, known for its dinosaur footprints, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1513649" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gollizo Pool (Jubera River)&lt;/a&gt; for a refreshing stop.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Santa Engracia Del Jubera?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's diverse terrain, from deep river valleys to steep rock formations, and the opportunity to explore historical mining landscapes. The preserved natural environment within the Biosphere Reserve is also a significant draw.
tips: A very cool hermitage, on the way to the Jubera mines.
A very cool environment, with caves, the entrance (closed today) to a mine and remains of the mine buildings. Very interesting.

One past the cave, accessed through a small tunnel and you reach a huge cavity with a large upper mouth, several entrances in the circumference, a small lake below and a waterfall that comes out of a spring. I loved it!
A delight to walk through this narrow ravine, sometimes on one side and sometimes on the other. Too bad it was quite dry.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Mina de la Providencia, Barranco de San Martín, Barranco Rojo circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 14.5 kilometers (9 miles) long and typically takes around 4 hours and 22 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace and how much time you spend exploring the highlights.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a moderate hike. It requires a good level of physical fitness and sure-footedness. While paths are mostly accessible, be prepared for varied terrain and an elevation gain and loss of about 480 meters (1,575 feet).

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several notable highlights. You'll encounter the historic Providencia Mine, which allows you to enter a large cave with a small lake and waterfall. The route also traverses the scenic Barranco de San Martín gorge and passes by the Hermitage of the Virgin of the Meadow.

faqs.question: Is the Mina de la Providencia, Barranco de San Martín, Barranco Rojo circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, given the varied terrain, including narrow sections in the barranco and potential cave exploration, ensure your dog is comfortable with such conditions and always keep them on a leash, especially in natural park areas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this route is typically during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder, potentially wet conditions, especially in the barranco and mine areas.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the natural park?
faqs.answer: The Mina de la Providencia, Barranco de San Martín, Barranco Rojo circular route is located within the Peñas de Iregua, Leza y Jubera Natural Park. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking the trail itself. However, always check local regulations for the natural park before your visit, especially regarding activities like cave exploration or specific access points.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details are not provided in the available information. As a circular route, you can typically start and end at the same point. It's advisable to check local maps or community forums for designated parking areas near the trailhead, which is often in a nearby village or a marked starting point for the route.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse, ranging from paths through the Barranco de San Martín gorge to areas around the Providencia Mine. Expect varied surfaces, including rocky sections, dirt paths, and potentially uneven ground, requiring sure-footedness.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route does intersect with other paths. Approximately 14% of the route covers sections of the official trail known as [CIMA LR05] Santa Marina.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's recommended to bring sturdy hiking boots, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and appropriate clothing layers. A headlamp or flashlight would be useful if you plan to explore inside the Providencia Mine.

faqs.question: Is there a specific strategy for exploring the Providencia Mine?
faqs.answer: When visiting the Providencia Mine, be aware that you enter a large cave through a small tunnel. Inside, there's a vast upper opening, multiple entrances, a small lake, and a waterfall. It's advisable to bring a reliable light source and exercise caution, as the interior can be dark and potentially slippery. Always prioritize safety and respect the natural environment.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of the Virgin of the Meadow
